entries.description: Temptation in the Garden of Eden: Deception of Adam and Eve leading to the Fall, original sin, expulsion from paradise, and introduction of death/suffering into the world (Genesis 3; Qur'an 7:20-25)
entries.causation_evidence: Hebrew Bible (Genesis), New Testament (Romans 5:12), Qur'an (Iblis/Satan refusal and temptation)

entries.description: Temptation of Jesus in the wilderness: Attempt to derail the Messiah's mission through pride, power, and provision (Matthew 4:1-11; Luke 4:1-13)
Inspiration for medieval witch hunts: Demonic possession accusations leading to torture and execution of tens of thousands (mostly women)
entries.causation_evidence: Malleus Maleficarum (1486), Inquisition records, Salem witch trials (1692)

entries.description: Modern Satanic Panic revivals (1980s–2020s): False ritual abuse accusations resulting in wrongful convictions, family destruction, and cultural fear
entries.causation_evidence: McMartin preschool case, West Memphis Three, Michelle Remembers hoax, QAnon-adjacent claims

entries.description: Theological justification for centuries of anti-Semitism (Jews as 'Christ-killers' or 'Satan's synagogue') fueling pogroms and expulsions
entries.causation_evidence: Medieval Church teachings, blood libel accusations, Reformation-era rhetoric

entries.description: Promotion of occultism, Satanism, and spiritual deception through modern media, music, and organizations
entries.causation_evidence: Church of Satan, The Satanic Temple, heavy metal/occult subcultures

entries.description: Inspiration for demonic possession narratives leading to exorcisms, torture, and deaths (e.g., Loudun possessions 1634, Anneliese Michel 1976)
Cultural glorification of rebellion, hedonism, and moral inversion in music, film, literature, and internet subcultures
entries.causation_evidence: Heavy metal, horror genres, Satanic imagery in pop culture

entries.description: Role in justifying inquisitions, crusades, and religious wars through accusations of demonic pacts and influence
Inspiration for modern Satanist activism and organizations (e.g., The Satanic Temple) that challenge religious authority and promote moral inversion
entries.causation_evidence: TST campaigns, Church of Satan writings

entries.description: Association with demonic possession leading to widespread fear, exorcisms, and torture in medieval Europe
entries.causation_evidence: Medieval demonology texts and trial records

entries.description: Promotion of moral relativism, nihilism, and rejection of divine authority in secular and subcultural movements
entries.causation_evidence: Philosophical and cultural critiques of Satanism's influence

entries.description: Inspiration for demonic pacts and black magic accusations leading to executions (e.g., Faust legend, witch trials)
entries.causation_evidence: Faust legend, European witch trial records

entries.description: Influence on modern conspiracy theories (Illuminati, New World Order, Satanic elite) fueling distrust and extremism
entries.causation_evidence: QAnon, Pizzagate, and related online movements

entries.description: Role in justifying inquisitions and religious wars through accusations of demonic influence on heretics
